nodes.title: Unique user accounts
nodes.raw_text: All user accounts must be uniquely attributable to one named individual. Shared or generic logins are prohibited except for documented, specifically-approved service accounts.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: unique, individually attributable user accounts; in order to: ensure every system action can be traced to one accountable person.

nodes.title: Multi-factor authentication for remote access
nodes.raw_text: Remote access to internal systems must require multi-factor authentication; a password alone is not sufficient.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: multi-factor authentication for remote access; in order to: prevent unauthorised access using a compromised password alone.

nodes.title: Periodic access review
nodes.raw_text: User access rights must be reviewed at least every six months, and any access no longer required must be revoked as a result of that review.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: six-monthly review of user access rights with revocation of unneeded access; in order to: stop unnecessary privileges accumulating over time.

nodes.title: Privileged account activity logging
nodes.raw_text: All actions performed under a privileged account must be logged, and the logs retained for at least twelve months.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: logging privileged account activity and keeping the logs for twelve months; in order to: support investigation of privileged misuse after the fact.

nodes.title: Least privilege
nodes.raw_text: Access to systems and data must be limited to the minimum necessary for a person to perform their role.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: granting only the minimum access needed for a person's role; in order to: limit the damage a single compromised account can do.

nodes.title: Timely removal of leaver access
nodes.raw_text: A person's access rights must be revoked no later than one working day after they leave the organisation or change to a role that no longer needs that access.
nodes.canonical_intent: requires: revoking access within one working day of someone leaving or changing role; in order to: prevent former staff or role-changers retaining access they should no longer have.
